programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Bring My Baby Home
programme.desc: A four-week-old girl is kidnapped, leaving her mother in anguish and police scrambling to find her. Joe Kenda and Robert Sapp fear she’s been sold on the black market… or worse.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Knock Knock
programme.desc: A gathering of friends turns deadly when two gunmen shoot William Davis in the head. Kenda and his team dig into his past and start to suspect someone in William’s circle is lying.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: My, My, Merry Christmas
programme.desc: Donald Ott is found shot to death, friends tell police he didn’t have an enemy in the world. But when Lt. Joe Kenda speaks with family, he learns of a relative who might hold the key to the mystery.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: I Will Hunt You Down
programme.desc: When two escaped inmates surface in Colorado Springs, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda and his Fugitive Unit to bring them to justice.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Run for your Life
programme.desc: A slain body of a marathon runner is found just hours before a big race. With the help of a trusted K-9 officer, Kenda sets off to find the killer himself before he strikes again.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: John 3:16
programme.desc: A man rushes his friend to the hospital with serious gunshot wounds, reporting they were robbed by strangers. When the facts don’t add up, Lt. Joe Kenda and his team go on the hunt.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Rearview Mirror
programme.desc: When a retired military man runs over a pedestrian, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda to determine if it was bad brakes or bad intent.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: A Prayer for the Dying
programme.desc: A mother of two is found dead in the most expensive part of Colorado Springs, leaving Lt. Joe Kenda stumped—until he looks into her personal life.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Tighten the Screws
programme.desc: When young Iraq War veteran Curtis Ashley ends up dead, Lt. Joe Kenda has to solve the slaying of a seemingly innocent victim using his keen awareness of the criminal mind.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: The Line Goes Dead
programme.desc: When the severed head of a wife and mother is found, Lt. Joe Kenda uses forensics and interrogation to find both her body and her killer.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: My Worst Fear
programme.desc: Police are shocked to find a 13-year-old boy killed in an explosion, but their grief turns to alarm when they discover a second, unexploded bomb.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Death Grip
programme.desc: When a speeding truck crashes into a residential building, officers find the lifeless body of Michael Faast behind the wheel. But he didn’t die in the wreck – he was shot point-blank in the temple.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: When police find 24-year-old Patrick Ray with a gunshot to the temple, the scene has all the telltale signs of suicide. But Lt. Joe Kenda's instinct says otherwise.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Rearview Mirror
programme.desc: When a retired military man runs over a pedestrian, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda to determine if it was bad brakes or bad intent.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Magic Bullet
programme.desc: A hard-working father of two is shot in the head just steps from a notorious strip club. The case is stuck in neutral until Kenda uncovers a conspiracy organized by an unlikely triggerman.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Snitch
programme.desc: When officers respond to a shooting , they discover the body of a young man they know all to well -- police informant Jimmie Stevenson. it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda to determine who pulled the trigger.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Dead On Target
programme.desc: A young father-to-be is shot dead in broad daylight by a single bullet fired from afar. At the crime scene, Kenda recognizes the hallmark of a skilled sniper.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: My, My, Merry Christmas
programme.desc: Donald Ott is found shot to death, friends tell police he didn’t have an enemy in the world. But when Lt. Joe Kenda speaks with family, he learns of a relative who might hold the key to the mystery.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: With Friends Like This
programme.desc: A young man is found gunned down outside his home . Sgt. Joe Kenda and Det. John Anderson must determine whether the killer was a complete stranger, a barrio gang member, or a jealous lover.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Bebe and Lola
programme.desc: English bulldog Bebe has turned against her pack mate, as well as the household furnishings. Comedian Howie Mandel and his wife, Terry, have their hands full with 2 year old Chihuahua Lola.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: UK Special 1
programme.desc: Cesar travels to the United Kingdom to help troubled dog owners. A teen struggles because his dogs go out of control on walks. And an adopted street dog from Romania is profiled.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Tori, Cassie & Skyler, and Tallulah, Bella, Boris, & Groucho, and Howie
programme.desc: An adopted miniature schnauzer compulsively spins in circles, four French bulldogs revolt against their owner, and an abused lab/chow/shepherd mix needs help accepting love.
programme.sub-title: Tori, Cassie & Skyler, and Tallulah, Bella, Boris, & Groucho, and Howie

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Amber, Snoopy, & Schroeder
programme.desc: A couple who can’t seem to control their high-strung Golden Retriever mix; Snoopy, a belligerent Bearded Collie; and Schroeder, an adopted a Lhasa Apso/Cavalier King Charles Spaniel mix with a bad case of separation anxiety.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Calvin, Rudy, and Milo
programme.desc: A ridgeback mix needs help recovering from a car accident that left him with three-legs; a manic Jack Russell chases motorcycles; and a border collie's herding instincts are out of control.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Josh & Boomer
programme.desc: Josh, a Maltese, will not let anyone with a brush or pair of scissors near him without putting up a fight. Boomer, a Chesapeake Bay Retriever, barks incessantly and refuses to come out of the pool.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Dasher, Riley, and Fosse
programme.desc: An Italian greyhound bites the hand that touches his toys; a 125-pound mastiff expands his grudge against the mailman; and a Yorkie has a shocking dislike of home appliances. Can Cesar help?

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Widow’s Dogs, Drool Dog, Dexter Follow-up
programme.desc: A retriever mix, and wire hair fox terriers siblings, have turned into a combative pack. A Sharpei/Dobie mix drools excessively. Rottie mix Dexter travels to Los Angeles for a refresher course.
programme.sub-title: Widow’s Dogs, Drool Dog, Dexter Follow-up

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Highlights from various Dog Whisperer stories that impart Cesar Millan’s advice for every stage of a dog’s lifespan. We follow old and new cases that show the process of adopting the right dog.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Maxwell, Brooklyn, and Tipper
programme.desc: A spoiled Welsh Sealyham Terrier has dominance issues; an excitable English Mastiff has behavioral issues, and a territorial Tibetan Terrier needs help accepting other dogs.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: J & Alice
programme.desc: J is unpredictable and aggressive and his owner is now dealing with a lawsuit. Alice is depressed and aggressive towards the other two dogs in the house – a potentially lethal combination.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Widow’s Dilemma
programme.desc: After Noelle’s husband unexpectedly passed away, their two dogs developed behavioral issues. The Wilson family’s Italian Greyhound Hula, snaps and growls at certain family members.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Roxie & Foxy, Charlie, and Bubba & Lucky
programme.desc: Two Dachshunds are overprotective of their owners. A couple wants to keep their biting Lhasa Apso. A couple working from home calls in Cesar for some canine conflict resolution.
programme.sub-title: Roxie & Foxy, Charlie, and Bubba & Lucky

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Sunshine & Teddy
programme.desc: Sunshine is a champion Doberman in the show ring but far too aggressive in the street. Teddy, a yellow lab, has bitten its owner twice, and has attacked more than one stranger.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Hyde & Vada and Nacho
programme.desc: Hyde and Vada, both wolf hybrids have aggression issues to work out especially when it comes to other dogs. Nacho, bit a child, and shows aggression when he has his toy.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: UK Special 2
programme.desc: Cesar’s adventures in the UK continue with unstable dogs and the people who love them. You’ll meet a dog walker, littermates who no longer get along, and a couple who can’t get their dog in the car.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A Min Pin turns ferocious. Actress Denise Richards' pug gets overprotective and aggressive towards visitors. A stubborn Bassett Hound has to literally be dragged places he doesn't want to go.
programme.sub-title: Chip, Leo, Hank, Lucy and Betty

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Cobar and Chase
programme.desc: Cesar takes a trip to Australia to help Cobar, a Koolie with a fear of squeaky toys. Then, he steps through the looking glass to help a Shar-pei mix who lunges at his own reflection in mirrors.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Sasha & Shep
programme.desc: Lhasa Apso Sasha, bares her teeth at anyone who takes anything from her. White German Shepherd, “Shep,” goes after strangers, making it impossible for his wheelchair bound owner to control.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Stanley, Shoven and Leo
programme.desc: Stanley, a surly Chihuahua mix, has a problem with attacking joggers. Two Pomeranians are known for their shrill barks. Shelter rescue Leo, performs a tight rope act when hearing a cough.

programme.title: Baywatch: Talk Show
programme.desc: Mitch gets his “15 minutes of fame” when he rescues Jay Leno from choking, and a female talk show host from drowning. Caroline and CJ adopt a stray dog swimming in the ocean that mystically reminds them of Stephanie.

programme.title: Baywatch: Life Guardian
programme.desc: After Caroline, Neely and Manny rescue three orphan teenage boys from drowning, Caroline seeks out the enigmatic, mysterious youth who helped her rescue one of the boys. Neely's ex-husband returns, complicating her life.

programme.title: Baywatch: Matters of the Heart
programme.desc: Manny is haunted by his past when two juvenile delinquents from his home borough in East LA arrive at Baywatch for community service. Meanwhile, Neely suspects Mitch and Samantha are becoming more than coworkers.

programme.title: Baywatch: Rendez-vous
programme.desc: When a young couple’s romance is threatened by their parents, they make a pact to end their lives together rather than end their romance. Meanwhile, Cody encounters a mermaid while diving, and of course no one believes him… except CJ.

programme.title: Baywatch: Hot Water
programme.desc: While testing new scuba diving technology, Sam, Neely and Caroline witness a jet crash. They rescue the pilot, who reveals that he was transporting a canister that contains a deadly virus. Meanwhile, the team must shut down an illegal nude beach.

programme.title: Baywatch: Baywatch at Sea World
programme.desc: Caroline finds herself being sued for negligence in civil court by the angry and bitter mother of a drowning victim. CJ babysits a neighbor’s newborn and realizes she wants to be a mother. But is Cody ready to be a father?

programme.title: Baywatch: Trial by Fire
programme.desc: CJ and Cody transport an injured sea lion to the animal recovery facility at Sea World. While there, Samantha gets the idea to promote lifeguarding by planning a Baywatch-theme stunt show at Sea World.

programme.title: Baywatch: Golden Girls
programme.desc: A crazed young woman is causing trouble on the Venice Boardwalk and Neely suspects it is more than meets the eye. Meanwhile, Newman meets his dream woman – who doesn’t speak English.

programme.title: Baywatch: Nevermore
programme.desc: A mysterious, disfigured man whom the public dubs "the phantom lifeguard," is secretly saving people and sending love notes to CJ. When CJ goes missing the team sets out to find her and the “phantom.”

programme.title: Baywatch: Rookie Summer
programme.desc: Young, ambitious and sexy lifeguard candidates compete for a chance to attend Rookie School. Mitch is offered a promotion to Captain and has a tough decision to make. Lani McKensie faces the sinister return of a former lover.

programme.title: Baywatch: Next Generation
programme.desc: Hobie, Lani, April and Skylar are among the trainees competing for a few open slots as Baywatch Lifeguards. Hobie isn't sure he wants to follow in his father's footsteps, but when a vengeful jet skier comes after April, Hobie comes to her rescue.

programme.title: Baywatch: The Choice
programme.desc: Veteran lifeguard Jack 'JD' Darius has a dark secret in his past. He comes to Baywatch to throw his rescue can in the ring for the open Lieutenant slot, but young and ambitious Taylor Walsh also wants the job, and she's ready to fight for it.

programme.title: Baywatch: Memorial Day
programme.desc: Mitch, Sky, and April find a dead man on the ocean floor. Their search for answers to his demise leads to the retirement home where he lived, and his two friends who seem to be hiding the true story. The chemistry between Cody and Lani heats up.

programme.title: Baywatch: Charlie
programme.desc: April is devastated by her younger brother Charlie’s terminal illness. Mitch and Hobie want to make sure Charlie's wishes are granted, and that the time he spends with the lifeguards at Baywatch is the best of his life.

programme.title: Baywatch: Lifeguard Confidential
programme.desc: Caroline's acting career isn't going well so she returns to Baywatch to figure out what she really wants to do with her life. The lifeguards are plagued by a sleaze-ball helicopter reporter who somehow knows intimate details about their love lives.

programme.title: Baywatch: Out of the Blue
programme.desc: After the devastation of the last big L.A. earthquake, Baywatch lifeguards are taking recent Seismic activity seriously. Caroline and JD 's tempestuous relationship heats up, forcing Caroline to make a critical decision that could change her life.

programme.title: Baywatch: Eel Nino
programme.desc: The hot Santa Ana winds blow an air of mystery to Baywatch when two sea lions perish in an unexplainable event. Mitch must uncover the cause to stop it from happening again. April is courted by Manny and Hobie, forcing her to choose between them.

programme.title: Baywatch: Homecoming
programme.desc: Donna tries to help Neely overcome her addiction to painkillers. Craig Pomeroy shows up at Baywatch to defend the legal rights of an elderly Native American shaman determined to initiate his tribe's traditional death ritual on the Baywatch beach.

programme.title: Baywatch: Missing
programme.desc: Cody ignores regulations and leaves his tower five minutes early to meet Lani for a hot date. Right after he leaves, a young woman goes swimming and is engulfed by a wave, never to reappear. Cody is tortured by the results of his negligence.

programme.title: Baywatch: Hijacked
programme.desc: Caroline's truck is hijacked by a young thief who forces her to drive him on a wild getaway chase. Neely reveals a shocking secret about her outrageous actions during her hiatus from Baywatch.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Cally and Lance discover how “Dark Oracle” came into their lives and attempt to get rid of it once and for all.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: The Dark Oracle comic returns with a vengeance and the Stone twins, Cally and Lance are more at risk than ever.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: The comic predicts that a red sports car will get into a terrible accident and when Cally's new crush, Emmett shows up at school with the very same car, Cally is horrified.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Evil Sage has taken real Sage's place and Lance and Cally are shocked when this new, uncensored Sage devastates Lance by hooking up with Vern.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Case of the Misguided Missal
programme.desc: A 15th century prayer volume disappears from a sealed casket inside a locked safe belonging to an Oxford Don; the supernatural is suspected.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Deadly Gift
programme.desc: A barmaid receives a gift, a music box from a thief, dead ten years. But someone is soon murdered and Colonel March believes the music holds the key.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Case of the Lively Ghost
programme.desc: A phony spiritualist believes she's truly summoned a real ghost. Colonel March attends her séance to discover the identity of the spirit's killer.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Death in the Dressing Room
programme.desc: Murder and blackmail culminate when a call for help during a Javenese dance is the clue that Colonel March needs to prove a faulty alibi.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The New Invisible Man
programme.desc: Colonel March receives a queer complaint: a pair of disembodied gloves killed a manand there is no corpse, no blood, and no evidence of wrongdoing,

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Passage At Arms
programme.desc: A woman is found dead, but was it suicide or murder? A French fencing match provides the telling clue.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Sorcerer
programme.desc: A psychologist is stabbed to death in a seemingly sealed room. Inspector March must find who killed him and how did they accomplish the task.

programme.title: The Last Man on Earth
programme.desc: When a disease turns all of humanity into the living dead, the last man on earth becomes a reluctant vampire hunter.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Strange Partners
programme.desc: A wealthy man uses extortion and a trained attack dog to force Peter to kill his partner and make it look like a heart attack.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Odds Against Death
programme.desc: A gangster forces one of Peter's staff to developed a mathematical system to win at the roulette table.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Picnic With Death
programme.desc: Peter's existence as an invisible man becomes public knowledge, while Sally's friend needs help when her stepfather plots to kill her invalid mother.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Play to Kill
programme.desc: A celebrated actress runs down a hobo by accident and a witness blackmails her, threatening to expose her secret.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: The Rocket
programme.desc: A top-secret rocket is compromised when one of the scientists runs into gambling debts. Peter heads out on a desperate search for the missing rocket.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Point of Destruction
programme.desc: Four tragic air accidents seem to point to the failure of a fuel diffuser being tested, but Peter suspects sabotage is at play.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Death Cell
programme.desc: An escaped mental patient comes to Peter, asking for his help in getting her fiancͩ cleared of a murder charge as he awaits execution.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Bride Possessed
programme.desc: Matt Conroy is startled when on their honeymoon his wide-eyed, Louisiana-drawling wife Sally suddenly becomes a determined and demanding woman with no accent who denies even knowing him.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Night of April 14th
programme.desc: A young Englishwoman is suddenly wracked by dreams of drowning in the ocean. She is stunned when her fiancée tells her that he has prepared a surprise honeymoon trip for them--they're sailing to New York on the passenger liner Titanic.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Emergency Only
programme.desc: A skeptic has his beliefs tested when events begin to unfold as a psychic had predicted.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Dark Room
programme.desc: A female photojournalist on assignment in France takes an apartment and begins to work with a somewhat mysterious male model.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Twelve Hours to Live
programme.desc: After her husband storms out of the house following an argument, his wife begins having visions of him in terrible danger.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Epilogue
programme.desc: A recovering alcoholic returns to his family from an extended stay in rehabilitation. His sobriety is put to the test, as are his beliefs in the supernatural, when his son is trapped in a cave-in.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Dream
programme.desc: In 1940, a fearful Britain awaits invasion by Nazi forces. A husband and his wife, separated by their wartime assignments, suddenly dream that each is in danger, and both try frantically to see if their dream has come true.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Premonition
programme.desc: Debbie is haunted by the fear of her own demise at the hands of a chandelier in her home.

programme.title: Backtrack
programme.desc: Psychologist Peter Bower's life is thrown into turmoil when he discovers that the patients he has been seeing are the ghosts of people who died twenty years ago.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: On Location
programme.desc: A film crew sets up shop on Bob and Margaret's street, giving them a small and unsavory taste of fame and fortune.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Stranded in Toronto
programme.desc: When Bob and Margaret take a trip to New York, their flight is re-routed to Toronto due to a storm.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Very Fishy Christmas
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret experience a truly Canadian Christmas.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Margaret Gets a Job
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have made the big move to Canada.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Strangers in a Strange Land
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret are mortified to discover that while they have been cooped up at Cookie and Melvin's house in Mississauga Ontario, their dogs have been left unattended by Penny, Bob's unreliable receptionist.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Undefined Border
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret go cross-border shopping in the United States.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Fish at the Bat
programme.desc: Bob gets cajoled into joining his office softball team.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Til Death Do Us
programme.desc: Bob gets conned into buying a cemetery plot for Margaret as a surprise 15th wedding anniversary gift.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: I, Bob
programme.desc: When an efficiency expert comes to assess the Inner City Dental Clinic, Bob gets side shifted out of a job.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Fly on the Wall
programme.desc: Feeling belittled by his family, Bob jumps at the chance to be the subject of a fly on the wall documentary.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A New Life
programme.desc: At a creepy chiropodist social night, Bob and Margaret encounter a couple who are rapturous about the new planned community where they now live.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Discomfort of Strangers
programme.desc: Margaret fears that her relationship with Bob is getting stale after they have a petty argument.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Player
programme.desc: Bob attends a personal finance seminar to help him get his hopeless financial house in order.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Mastermind
programme.desc: When Bob and Margaret have a fender-bender with a local mobster, they accidentally turn him into the police.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Party Politics
programme.desc: Margaret runs into a distant friend who asks her to have an Ann Summers-type party in her home.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Getaway
programme.desc: Margaret is nominated for the Golden Arch Support award for outstanding service in the field of footcare.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: No Trouble
programme.desc: This charming, witty animated series focuses on the ordinary lives of a middle class dentist and his foot doctor wife. Together, they tackle traumas as being burgled, going on a holiday and deciding whether or not having a child will ruin their lives.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Blood, Sweat and Tears
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret decide they need to get fit.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Holiday
programme.desc: This charming, witty animated series focuses on the ordinary lives of a middle class dentist and his foot doctor wife. Together, they tackle traumas as being burgled, going on a holiday and deciding whether or not having a child will ruin their lives.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Cottage Country
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret get invited up to the cottage of Dr. Yoselheiffer, President of the Canadian Council of Dentists.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Night In
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret find themselves at loose ends one evening when, to their dismay, they find that there is absolutely nothing to watch on TV.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Trouble with Mummy
programme.desc: Bob is concerned when he hears that his mother's best friend Pearl has passed on.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Wedding
programme.desc: Cookie and Melvin invite Bob and Margaret to the wedding of their goddaughter, Tiffany.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Cuckoo in the Nest
programme.desc: When Bobs old friend Clive, (who is a real drunken slob), is dumped by his wife, Bob lets him stay for a few days despite Margaret's objections?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Gone to Seed
programme.desc: Bob decides to donate his sperm to a local sperm bank.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Driving Bob
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have put it off for too long. They need to get their Canadian drivers' licenses.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: My Foot Hurts
programme.desc: Margaret gets her own local call-in radio show entitled My Foot Hurts. On the first day, nervous and short on callers, she mentions her favourite curry house on the air?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Going Dutch
programme.desc: Margaret enters a contest and wins the grand prize: a cycling tour of Holland!

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: For Peter's Sake
programme.desc: Bob's brother Peter is a successful chef who has a cooking show producing in India.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Problems
programme.desc: At her clinic one-day, Margaret sees an old school chum, and invites her and her husband Matt to dinner.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Neighbours
programme.desc: In this story of neighbourhood social angst, Bob is having a complex burglar alarm installed by Graham, a sad, lonely man whose confidence is lifted immeasurably by this rare sale to Bob.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Shopping
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret, concerned at the amount they are spending in take away food and expensive local food shops, decide on an expedition to the Super Save Shopatropolis in Croydon.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Life Saver
programme.desc: Bob inadvertently saves the life of a suicidal man who then decides to become Bob's best friend.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Dental Convention
programme.desc: The time for the annual dental convention has come. Bob is not keen on going when he sees that a colleague from his student days, Bernard Wiggins, a "loud mouthed big shot", is a featured speaker.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Bob Gets Wired
programme.desc: When Margaret forgets to pay for some sprinkles at the local cake shop, she is hauled in as a shoplifter.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Book Club
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret join a book club in the hopes of expanding their tiny social horizon.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Animal Behaviour
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret arrive home from another boring evening out with dreary friends, to find that their dogs behaviour has taken a turn for the worse?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Candidate
programme.desc: Margaret finds herself inadvertently running for City Council when the candidate she is supporting drops out of the race.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Gary
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret thought they knew everything about their next door neighbours, until Joyce and Trevor introduce them to their grown son, Gary.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: New Lease on Life
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have near-death experience when Bob nearly crashes their car.

programme.title: On the Case: A Cracked Alibi
programme.desc: If investigators can't find the crack in their suspect's seemingly air-tight alibi, a cold-blooded killer will elude justice.

programme.title: On the Case: Murder by the Numbers?
programme.desc: A small town is outraged when its unofficial mayor is the victim of a vicious attack at work. The police investigation uncovers a number of possible suspects and a confusing trail of evidence but will it lead them to the person who committed the crime?

programme.title: On the Case: Darkness in The Desert
programme.desc: Paula Zahn investigates the story of a Las Vegas starlet who mysteriously vanished during a visit to a local spy store.

programme.title: On the Case: In Defiance of Evil
programme.desc: A 13-year-old does not return from her daily bike ride, and three weeks later, joggers make a grim discovery. Police connect her case to another girl's abduction and murder five months earlier, but it will take decades to uncover the truth.

programme.title: On the Case: Greed, Lies and Murder
programme.desc: Fifty-year-old mother of three Marilou Johnson disappears from the home of her boyfriend, Roger Blanchard, a wealthy retired businessman, then it becomes clear Johnson has met with foul play.

programme.title: On the Case: Scattered Clues, Shattered Lives
programme.desc: January 10th 1984. A frigid morning when pilot Ron Holm arrived home from a long flight. As Ron pulled into his Aurora Colorado driveway, he was puzzled to see that the garage door was open and his girlfriend purse was on the ground.

programme.title: On the Case: In the Blink of an Eye
programme.desc: November 21, 1988, Sheriff's deputies received an alarming phone call from a convenience store in Leesville, Louisiana. A delivery person said the clerk who signs for deliveries was missing and the store's cash register was open and empty
programme.sub-title: In the Blink of an Eye

programme.title: On the Case: Lying in Wait
programme.desc: A young mother goes out for a jog and runs into her killer. Does her teenage daughter hold the clue to solve the case?

programme.title: On the Case: This is for Her
programme.desc: A deadly home invasion leaves a father of three dead. Why was the victim's wife, the only eyewitness to the crime left alive?

programme.title: On the Case: The Killer Rang Twice
programme.desc: Linda Donahew, of Arlington Texas, was discovered stabbed to death by her sister one night in June. For nearly two decades the case remained unsolved, until detectives matched DNA from Linda's case with another case a few hundred miles away.

programme.title: On the Case: Classified Murder
programme.desc: A couple answers a classified ad to buy a collectible motorcycle, but is never seen again. The ad turns out to be an untraceable fake, and the case will go cold for years until an unlikely witness comes forward.

programme.title: On the Case: A Killer in Town
programme.desc: Paula Zahn examines how police raced to track down a vicious killer on his deadly cross-country journey.

programme.title: On the Case: Hidden in Plain Sight
programme.desc: A nine year old girl scout disappears while delivering cookies to a neighbor.

programme.title: On the Case: Long Walk, Cold Night
programme.desc: When a teenage girl is found murdered after a night out with friend, Police must retrace her final steps to find her killer.

programme.title: On the Case: In Broad Daylight
programme.desc: A young mother is murdered in broad daylight with her toddler just a few feet away. Police have little to go on except the bizarre description eyewitnesses have given them of the brazen killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Unmasking a Killer
programme.desc: A beautiful grad student is found murdered inside a yoga apparel store. On the surface it appeared she was the victim of a botched robbery, but remarkable police work revealed a far more sinister plot.

programme.title: On the Case: Silenced
programme.desc: When a beautiful young woman goes missing, police are baffled.

programme.title: On the Case: Half Way Home
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body inside an abandoned trailer leads to a 28-year search for the person responsible for her beating death.

programme.title: On the Case: Lost In The Fire
programme.desc: When a pregnant mother of two is found shot to death in her fire-ravaged home, police find there are more suspects than clues.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: A Dark and Stormy Night (REDUX)
programme.desc: Darius reconciles with Luc, while making peace with Dante, but the sinister forces that follow him are not far behind.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Supermax (REDUX)
programme.desc: After Rudolpho sells the Tulip out from under them, the crew scheme to take it back from the new owner, who wants to turn the ship into a maximum security prison.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: A Twist in Time (REDUX)
programme.desc: While transporting an insane convict named "Five," Dante diverts course to answer a distress call from a graviton research station on Triton, which is experimenting with the distortion of space and time.

programme.title: Black Hole High: Technology
programme.desc: An old box of Pearadyne Lab computer chips leads Marshall into a moneymaking scheme, but he's accused of cheating. Also the students of Blake Holsey go cellphone and texting crazy.

programme.title: Black Hole High: Equation
programme.desc: The Academic Olympics team trains under Capt. Corrine (who is obsessed with winning). Josie, Lucas, Marshall, and Vaughn must figure out why they are doing things they normally couldn't or shouldn't be doing.

programme.title: Black Hole High: Hemispheres
programme.desc: When Corrine auditions for a band as a singer, she looks at a mirror, through which she travels. To return, Corrine must learn to use the creative half of her brain without losing her ability to use her logical side.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Midnight Flight
programme.desc: Germania, ca. 400 AD: an artifact called Gunther's Scepter is sealed in a vault. Kleine Burgh, 2001: Sydney and Nigel are hired to find it using a crystal and a stone tablet.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: The Executioner's Mask
programme.desc: When a model's face is disfigured after she puts on the mask of a French executioner, Sydney and Nigel will try and find the tomb to reverse the curse.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: The Royal Ring
programme.desc: When the British Museum gets Sidney and Nigel to help search at Tudor sites for a ring of King Henry VIII, Nigel is confronted with his self-confident pestering elder brother Preston Bailey.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Set in Stone
programme.desc: In a monastery, Knight Gabriel impales a demon, which then turns to stone. In 2001, Nigel and Sydney arrive bearing a letter from a monk, but the abbot claims it is a forgery and shuts them out.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Deadline
programme.desc: Allan comes to Sydney, telling he has 12 hours before a Necron virus kills him unless he delivers Tau to a blackmailer who has the antidote.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Wages of Sydney
programme.desc: Quan Shu, Manchuria 1359 BC: The Chinese invent an explosive gift egg. New York, 2001: A rival beats Sydney and Nigel to a map leading to the Dragon's Egg.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Vampire's Kiss
programme.desc: Lucas Blackmer, a babe-magnet, professor and a novelist asks Sydney to help find Vlad Tepes' lost relic. And he isn't the only one after it.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Mr. Right
programme.desc: Sydney runs into her ex Gray and goes on a holiday with him to Bali, but when Derek Lloyd shows up to enlist her to find Parvati's Bowl, she can't resist.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Sydney at Ten
programme.desc: Sydney's roommate from school is on track of a pendant from her past and enlists Sydney to help find it and catch the killer.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: The Light of Truth
programme.desc: Arabia 843 AD: A sheik uses the Light of Truth to reveal his general as a traitor. 2001: Prince Shareem drops out from Sydney's class to go search for the Light of Truth.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Treasure Island
programme.desc: Sydney's on the hunt for a Pirate's booty, but will a traitor force her to walk the plank?

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S2 E9 - Out Of Their Minds
programme.desc: An alien ship fires a weapon at Moya. The blast makes it through and causes everyone's consciousness to jump into another body.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E9 - Out Of Their Minds

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S2 E10 - My Three Crichtons
programme.desc: A strange globe appears on Moya, sucking in Crichton and duplicating him creating two additional people.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E10 - My Three Crichtons

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S2 E11 - Look At The Princess, Part 1: A Kiss Is But A Kiss
programme.desc: On a planet about to crown its new monarch. The problem is the Princess cannot ascend unless she finds a genetically compatible mate.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E11 - Look At The Princess, Part 1: A Kiss Is But A Kiss

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S2 E12 - Look At The Princess, Part 2: I Do, I Think
programme.desc: The Queen offers Crichton a choice; marry her daughter the crown princess, or she'll turn him over to Scorpius.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E12 - Look At The Princess, Part 2: I Do, I Think

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Garry Newlove
programme.desc: Garry Newlove’s wife Helen talks about the events on the night of his murder and the days afterwards, as well as her current work as a peer in the House of Lords combatting anti-social behaviour.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Sutcliffe
programme.desc: Arguably Britain's most infamous serial killer, Peter Sutcliffe became known as The Yorkshire Ripper when he began murdering women in Yorkshire during the 1970s.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Christopher Foster
programme.desc: Failed businessman Christopher Foster killed his wife and daughter before burning their mansion in Shropshire to the ground in August 2008.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rolf Harris
programme.desc: Rolf Harris became famous for his artwork and music, even painting a portrait of the Queen for the jubilee. But it all came crumbling down in August 2013 when he was accused of 12 assaults on girls as young as eight years old between 1968 and 1986.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Dale Cregan
programme.desc: It's 2012 and drug dealer Dale Cregan murders police officers Nicola Hughes and Fiona Bone. Dermot Murnaghan investigates.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Angus Sinclair
programme.desc: Angus Sinclair, called Scotland's worst serial killer, is linked to the 1977 murders of two girls in Edinburgh.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Moors Murders
programme.desc: An investigation into the Moors Murderers, Myra Hindley and Ian Brady, with interviews with the family of Keith Bennett.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Suzy Lamplugh
programme.desc: A look at the case of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh, who showed a client around a house in Fulham in July 1986 and was never seen again. Dermot Murnaghan hosts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Wrongly Released
programme.desc: This special episode shines a light on criminals who have been brought to justice for one crime only to be released to commit further terrible acts.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Time Of Death: Albert Walker
programme.desc: When a Devonshire fisherman hauled in his nets on July 28 1996, he made a gruesome discovery: among that day’s catch was a dead body. There was nothing to identify the man’s body except a Rolex watch and a small maple leaf tattoo on his hand.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Murder By Mail: Keith Cottingham
programme.desc: In 1984 a parcel bomb was sent to a 53-year-old grandmother living in a tranquil English village. She died of horrific injuries. No one understood why it had happened. The only evidence detectives had was the bomb itself.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Hitman And The Hairdresser: Fadi Nasri
programme.desc: A 29-year-old hairdresser was found brutally stabbed on her doorstep in a quiet suburban street of London. A recording of a threatening phone call seemed to suggest the motive, but every lead comes to a dead end.
programme.sub-title: The Hitman And The Hairdresser: Fadi Nasri

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: DNA Of A Murder: Andrew George
programme.desc: Hilda Murrell, a 78-year-old distinguished rose-grower, was found dead in woodland near her home town. Despite a huge investigation by the British police, detectives failed to find her killer and conspiracy theories muddied the waters still further.
programme.sub-title: DNA Of A Murder: Andrew George

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Body In The Suitcase: Lorenzo Simon
programme.desc: On 12th May 2014 a human torso was discovered in a suitcase during routine maintenance work on a Birmingham canal. With no useable DNA and no other body parts, the case initially seemed impossible to crack.
programme.sub-title: The Body In The Suitcase: Lorenzo Simon

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Bodies Of Evidence: Roger Severs
programme.desc: In November 1993, Derek and Eileen Severs disappeared without a word. When police spoke to their son, Roger, he insisted they were on holiday. However, there was no evidence of this apparent trip, and Roger was soon arrested on suspicion of murder.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan returns to uncover the truth behind the horrific crimes of Peterborough serial killer Joanna Dennehy.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Keith Blakelock
programme.desc: In 1985, PC Keith Blakelock was murdered during a riot on a housing estate in London, triggered after a black woman died of heart failure during a house search. Nobody has been brought to justice for his murder, despite three investigations.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Hannegret Donnelly
programme.desc: The Donnelly family’s neighbours knew they were unusual. Christopher and Hannegret were rarely seen outside the house, and the children were home-schooled. But no one could guess the violence taking place within their home - until it was too late.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Lois Riess
programme.desc: Lois and David Riess were grandparents and business owners. Lois was also a gambling addict who would do anything to feed her habit. David became the first victim of a murderous rampage that earned Lois the title of America’s most wanted grandmother.

programme.title: Strangest Things: German Codes, Egyptian Helicopters And The Future Machine
programme.desc: Is a device that looks like a typewriter in a wooden box really one of the most dangerous weapons in history? Why is there a modern helicopter carved in an ancient Egyptian temple? Can an 800-year-old Arabic machine really calculate your future?
programme.sub-title: German Codes, Egyptian Helicopters And The Future Machine

programme.title: Strangest Things: The Viking Coin, The Ancient Disk And The Dead Girl That Saved A Million Lives
programme.desc: Can a shred of metal dug up on a thousand-year-old native American site really be Viking? Is a strange fired clay disc discovered on Crete really the product of Europe’s earliest civilisation, or is it a fake?
programme.sub-title: The Viking Coin, The Ancient Disk And The Dead Girl That Saved A Million Lives

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Mystery Of Glacier Mummy
programme.desc: Mountain climbers in California find the body of a U.S. Army soldier. In Alaska, strange bundles of canvas and wood high up on a mountain are discovered. Volcanic glass is found in an unexpected place. A vital WWII weather station is uncovered.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Siberia's Frozen Necropolis
programme.desc: Researchers find a mummy buried in a strange way. A legendary polar exploration vehicle goes missing in Antarctica. A glacier cave on Mt. St. Helens is explored. An island in the Bering Sea may contain clues to the extinction of the woolly mammoth.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Secrets Of The Ice Arsenal
programme.desc: An unidentified metal object is found on Kiska Island. A centuries-old gold mining mystery is investigated. In Siberia, a network of ice tunnels is explored. Fossils unearthed in the Canadian Arctic lead to the discovery of a new species of camel.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Russia's Mountain Mummy Enigma
programme.desc: Russian soldiers make a grim discovery on a mountain. An abandoned town has an unusual past. Scientists working in Antarctica find something surprising beneath the ice. In Greenland, strange depressions in the ground are investigated.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Buried Secrets Of Mount Cook
programme.desc: Merging CGI recreations, advanced scanning technology and scientific research, this series journeys to the planet’s most remote, cold and inhospitable places to reveal great mysteries that were once frozen in time.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Freaks Of Nature

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Medical Mysteries

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Power Of The Mind
programme.desc: Power of the Mind: A housewife has a strange and sudden vision that helps to solve a murder. A man remembers every single moment of his life.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Ancient Mysteries

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Amazing Survival

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Man Beasts

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Aliens Walk Among Us
programme.desc: Stories that suggest aliens are among us, including an Arizona woman who claims to have been fathered by an alien in a top-secret government experiment.

programme.desc: Curses: Do cursed objects really exist? This episode explores a painting in Britain that is said to start deadly house fires, a sinister doll in Florida and a curse-proof house.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Man Beasts
programme.desc: Man Beasts: We're all fascinated by mysteries. But is the unexplained really unexplainable? William Shatner investigates in an attempt to find a logical explanation.

programme.desc: This week, the spotlight is on the chefs from Wales – Richard Davies, Aled Williams and James Sommerin. Today they kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks? Former champion Stephen Terry will decide.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Fish
programme.desc: Today the chefs from Wales – Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Aled Williams - pull out all the stops with their fish dishes: poached lobster with confit potatoes, sea bass with brown crab, and sea trout with apple, beetroot and smoked mussels.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Main
programme.desc: Today the chefs from Wales battle it out with their main courses. Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Aled Williams are determined to win, but which dish will take the top spot today?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Dessert
programme.desc: Today is the last chance for the chefs from Wales to impress. Richard Davies, Aled Williams and James Sommerin deliver their desserts, but which dish will earn top marks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Wales. One chef left the competition yesterday and only two remain as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Starter
programme.desc: This week the spotlight is on the chefs from the North East – Kenny Atkinson, Tim Bilton and Lee Bennett. Today they kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Fish
programme.desc: Today the chefs from the North East – Kenny Atkinson, Tim Bilton and Lee Bennett pull out all the stops with their fish dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Main
programme.desc: Today the chefs from the North East battle it out with their main courses. Kenny Atkinson, Tim Bilton and Lee Bennett are determined to win but which dish will take the top spot today?

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Desssert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from the North East to impress as the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition. Kenny Atkinson, Tim Bilton and Lee Bennett deliver their desserts, but which dish will earn top marks

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Judging
programme.desc: Its decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the North East. One chef left the competition yesterday and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Starter
programme.desc: This week the spotlight is on the chefs from Northern Ireland – Niall McKenna, Derek Creagh and Brian McCann. Today they kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Northern Ireland. One chef left the competition yesterday and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Starter
programme.desc: It's the turn of the chefs from London and the South East - Tristan Welch, Tom Kerridge and Anthony Demetre. Today they're out to impress with their starters; breast of lamb with crisp lambs' fry; beer-braised oxtail and ham, egg and chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Fish
programme.desc: Today the chefs from the London and South East region – Tom Kerridge, Tristan Welch and Anthony Demetre want to score top marks with their fish dishes: wild brown trout with onion tart; crayfish Scotch egg; and brown trout, crayfish and watercress pie.

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Main
programme.desc: Today it's the battle of the main courses, as the chefs from London and the South East – Tom Kerridge, Tristan Welch and Anthony Demetre – face off. Which dish will come out on top?

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Dessert
programme.desc: Today it's the last chance for the chefs from London and the South East – Anthony Demetre, Tom Kerridge and Tristan Welch - to prove they've got what it takes. So which dessert will former champion Jason Atherton think is worthy of The Prince?

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Judging
programme.desc: It's judgment day on Great British Menu. One chef left the competition yesterday and now only two remain to battle it out, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Final Starter
programme.desc: It's the finals of Great British Menu. In today's program, the finalists cook their starters, which will be tasted and scored by the Great British Menu judges, and in a twist to the competition, there's a new addition to the judging panel.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Final Fish
programme.desc: Only 8 chefs remain and they are all determined to get at least one of their dishes through to the banquet. Today they cook their fish dishes for the Great British Menu judges, who are joined by a fourth member to assist them in their decision.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Final Main
programme.desc: It's day 3 of the finals of Great British Menu, when the panel of judges give their verdict on the main courses. 8 of the country's top chefs are competing to cook at a banquet, celebrating the very best local produce from around the British Isles.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Tamron Hall
programme.desc: Tamron Hall's tenacious personality shines through as Bear pushes her to her limits.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Courtney Cox
programme.desc: Actress Courteney Cox joins Bear to challenge herself into sharp edged discomfort amidst the jagged peaks and descending cliffs on the windswept coast of Ireland.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Sterling K. Brown
programme.desc: Actor Sterling K. Brown joins Bear in the high mountains of Colorado, where they battle plummeting temperatures while descending a sheer rock face, track an elusive deer and rappel down a waterfall.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Shaquille O'Neal
programme.desc: Bear and NBA legend Shaquille O'Neal are lifted from a freight train via helicopter and whisked away to a dense forest in the remote Adirondacks; they face blood-sucking leeches, and a 200-foot rock face as they search for their exit.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Deon Sanders
programme.desc: Bear Grylls takes NFL hall of famer Deion Sanders to the mesas and canyons of Utah for his first ever camping trip.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Nick Jonas
programme.desc: Renowned survivalist Bear Grylls is accompanied by singer Nick Jonas on another one of his journeys into the wilderness.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Don Cheadle
programme.desc: Actor Don Cheadle joins Bear on a trek through the mountains of New England, which includes crossing a raging river and a climb to a towering railroad bridge. Bear considers a porcupine carcass for dinner.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Channing Tatum
programme.desc: Channing Tatum hikes, explores and climbs during his expedition to Yosemite National Park. The pair begin with a helicopter ride, move through dense forest, then eat some dangerous food.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Julianne Hough
programme.desc: Bear and actress Julianne Hough navigate the unforgiving terrain and wild animals of the African savanna; Julianne talks about her adolescence and her determination to succeed.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Lindsey Vonn
programme.desc: Bear and Olympic gold medallist Lindsey Vonn take a speedboat trip, then swim to shore and make their way along a rugged coastline; after traversing a deep chasm, they collect a dinner of sea urchins.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Lena Headey
programme.desc: Lena Headey and Bear journey through Spain's Iberian Peninsula; they rappel from a towering ridge, dine on bone marrow from a goat carcass and cross a lake using a makeshift raft during an unexpected storm; Lena details her life and rise to stardom.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Roger Federer
programme.desc: Bear takes one of the best athletes of all time, Roger Federer, on a daring adventure through the Swiss Alps as they attempt to navigate the tough terrain. They descend a frozen waterfall and use tennis rackets as snowshoes.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, James Marsden
programme.desc: X-man' James Marsden joins Bear on an adventure into the heart Rattle Snake Canyon in Utah, where they helicopter on to one of the highest arches in the area.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Drew Brees
programme.desc: Bear Grylls ventures deep into Panama's Camino de Cruces jungle with Super Bowl winning quarterback Drew Brees, exploring caves and wrestling a six-foot crocodile.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Ed Helms
programme.desc: Actor Ed Helms faces sheer cliffs, crumbling rock and a cringeworthy dinner menu while atop Colorado mountains; Helms discusses the road to his career in comedy.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Joseph Gordon-Levitt
programme.desc: Bear and Joseph Gordon-Levitt navigate Kenya's unyielding terrain; Joseph and Bear navigate a perilous rappel, milk a camel for hydration, ascend a massive rock dome and battle a crocodile for dinner; Joseph talks about his family and rise to fame.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Zac Efron
programme.desc: Hollywood actor Zac Efron heads into the Appalachian wilderness of the Catskill Mountains in New York. They parachute into the woods, rappel down cliffs and search for shelter.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Mel B
programme.desc: Spice Girl Mel B joins Bear on the two hundred foot cliffs of the Welsh coastline, where a final descent sends Mel into breakdown mode; Bear is stung by a jellyfish, forcing Mel to practice some first aid skills.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Ben Stiller
programme.desc: Ben Stiller travels to Northern Scotland to face the Isle of Skye's rugged mountains. Stiller discusses his background while they navigate slippery terrain, searching for food and clean water.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Derek Hough
programme.desc: Dancer Derek Hough joins Bear on an adventure across the mountains of Bulgaria; Derek reveals childhood struggles and teaches Bear some dance moves.
